sections.paragraph:
ἀντεῖπον 2 aor. (used in place of the missing aor. of ἀντιλέγω, q.v.; but ἀντιείπατε Just., D. 71, 3, cp. ἀντεῖπας Rv 2:13 v.l. [if the unaccented form in Tdf. app. is to be read as a verb] and s. Borger, TRu 52, ’87, 45–47); fut. ἀντερῶ LXX (Aeschyl., Thu. et al.; LXX; Jos., Ant. 1, 11; 19, 208; SibOr 2, 276) to say someth. in reply, say in return ἀ. τινί (PMich 219, 9) contradict someone Lk 21:15; Hm 3:3. οὐδὲν εἶχον ἀντειπεῖν they had nothing to say in reply Ac 4:14 (cp. Aeschyl., Prom. 51 οὐδὲν ἀντειπεῖν ἔχω; POxy 237 V, 13 [186 a.d.] ἐσιώπησεν οὐδὲν ἀντειπεῖν δυνάμενος). ἀντιεῖπεν ὁ Ἰωσὴφ λέγων GJs 9:2.—DELG s.v. ἔπος.

aor. aor. = aorist

q.v. q.v. = quod vide (which see, see that which has just been mentioned)

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Tdf. Tdf. = CvTischendorf—List 1, beg.

app. app. = appendix, apparatus (when ref. to marginal text-critical information, esp. in N.)

TRu TRu = Theologische Rundschau—List 6

fut. fut. = fut.

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

someth. someth. = something

PMich PMich = Michigan Papyri—List 4

Hm Hm = Mandates

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 87
